name: frontend-security-coder
description: Expert in secure frontend coding practices specializing in XSS
capabilities: Generate XSS-safe code patterns for JavaScript and framework code
Identify potential security vulnerabilities in frontend code
Create CSRF token implementations and validation logic
Produce Content Security Policy headers and configurations
Generate input sanitization functions for user inputs
Provide secure authentication flow guidance for frontend apps
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill that generates frontend security content. No executable code was detected. The skill provides security guidance through natural language prompts without any system-level access, network calls, or file operations. Risk score is 0/100.
valueStatement: Frontend applications are vulnerable to XSS, CSRF, and other attacks. This skill generates secure code patterns and identifies vulnerabilities in your web applications to protect against common attack vectors.
limitations: Does not scan or analyze existing codebases - only generates content from prompts
Cannot detect backend vulnerabilities or server-side issues
Does not perform live security testing or penetration testing
Security recommendations are based on patterns, not runtime analysis

useCases.title: Secure Component Development
useCases.description: Generate security-aware React, Vue, or Angular components with built-in XSS protection and input validation

useCases.targetUser: Security engineers reviewing web application code
useCases.title: Security Code Review
useCases.description: Get detailed security analysis of frontend code snippets with specific vulnerability identification and remediation guidance

useCases.targetUser: Junior developers learning web security fundamentals
useCases.title: Security Best Practices Learning
useCases.description: Learn secure coding patterns and practices for frontend development through generated examples and explanations

promptTemplates.title: Basic XSS Protection
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a XSS-safe input component in [FRAMEWORK] that sanitizes user input and prevents script injection attacks. Include proper escaping functions.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a secure input field that protects against cross-site scripting

promptTemplates.title: CSRF Token Handler
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a CSRF token management module for a frontend application. Include token retrieval, validation on requests, and automatic token refresh logic.

promptTemplates.title: Secure Authentication Flow
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a secure authentication flow pattern for a single-page application. Include token storage best practices, refresh token handling, and session timeout logic.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building secure login and session management

promptTemplates.title: CSP Configuration Guide
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a comprehensive Content Security Policy configuration for a modern web application. Include directives for scripts, styles, images, and API calls.
promptTemplates.scenario: Setting up Content Security Policy headers

bestPractices: Always validate and sanitize user input on both client and server sides, even if client-side validation is present
Use modern security libraries like DOMPurify for HTML sanitization rather than writing custom escaping functions
Implement Content Security Policy headers to provide an additional layer of defense against XSS attacks
antiPatterns: Using innerHTML with user-provided data without sanitization - this creates direct XSS vulnerabilities
Storing sensitive tokens in localStorage instead of httpOnly cookies - tokens are accessible to JavaScript
Disabling CSRF protection for API endpoints because it adds complexity - this exposes the application to cross-site request forgery

faq.question: Does this skill scan my existing codebase for vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates security-focused code and patterns from prompts. It does not analyze external codebases.

faq.question: Can this skill replace a security audit?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides coding guidance and patterns. It cannot replace professional security audits or penetration testing.

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill can generate code for React, Vue, Angular, vanilla JavaScript, and other popular frontend frameworks.

faq.question: Does the generated code work with both frontend and backend?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on frontend security patterns. Backend security requires server-side implementations not covered by this skill.

faq.question: How do I use this with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Activate the skill and provide specific prompts about the security pattern you need. The skill will generate appropriate code and explanations.

faq.question: Are the security recommendations up to date?
faq.answer: The skill generates recommendations based on established security practices. Always verify against current security standards and OWASP guidelines.
